chunk_summary: This section focuses on dietary therapy for protein-losing enteropathy and intestinal lymphangiectasia, emphasizing tailored nutrition strategies to manage malabsorption and associated complications in GI disorders.
learning_objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of protein-losing enteropathy and intestinal lymphangiectasia.
Identify dietary modifications to manage malabsorption in these conditions.
Explore guidelines for nutrition support in pediatric GI disorders.
Recognize risks such as refeeding syndrome during nutritional interventions.
key_facts: Primary intestinal lymphangiectasia involves impaired lymphatic drainage leading to protein loss and malabsorption.
Dietary therapy often includes low-fat, high-protein diets supplemented with medium-chain triglycerides (MCTs).
NASPGHAN recommends tailored nutrition support for children with chronic liver disease and GI malabsorption.
Refeeding syndrome is a critical risk during nutritional rehabilitation, requiring careful monitoring of electrolytes and caloric intake.
Management strategies may include fat-soluble vitamin supplementation due to malabsorption.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Primary intestinal lymphangiectasia is characterized by impaired lymphatic drainage, leading to protein loss and malabsorption. Dietary therapy includes low-fat, high-protein diets with MCT supplementation. (Lopez et al, J Paediatr Child Health 2020)
document_key_topics: Nutrition support in pediatric GI disorders
